rulings.Case Title: CAVALRY PORTFOLIO SERVICES, LLC, AS ASSIGNEE OF VS. HARRY SLOMOVITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption (Wage Garnishment - Enforcement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 21, 2012, Line 1. PLAINTIFF CAVALRY PORTFOLIO SERVICES, LLC, AS ASSIGNEE OF CITIBANK, N.A.'S Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption. Granted. The judgment creditor has failed to provide any evidence of the amount of any post-judgment interest that has accrued since November 28, 2007. In particular, there is no indication that the required section 685.050 declaration was filed in connection with the February 2, 2012 writ of execution. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WILSON POWELL et al VS. DOUGLAS R. LIVINGSTON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Three, And For Monetary Sanctions Against Deft; Pos
rulings.Rulings: set for hearing on monday, May 21, 2012, Line 2. PLAINTIFF WILSON POWELL, PACIFIC SIGNALING SYSTEMS, INC.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Three, And For Monetary Sanctions Against defendant. Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KYLE HEINZ VS. PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Final Approval Of Class Action Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 21, 2012, Line 10. PLAINTIFF KYLE HEINZ'S Notice Of Motion For Final Approval Of Class Action Settlement. Granted in part. All parts of the settlement agreement are approved in their entirely except the provisions regarding attorney's fees and costs. The attorneys' fees are reduced to $127,500 which reflects a multiplier of 1.5. That multiplier is appropriate given the contingent risk and the results achieved. All claimed costs of $9,792 are awarded. The total of fees and costs is $137,292. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DOROTHY JEAN BENNETT VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Form Ingterrogatories
rulings.Rulings: set for hearing on monday, May 21, 2012, Line 4. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Compel Responses To Form interrogatories. Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant City and County of San Francisco's unopposed motion to compel is granted. Plaintiff shall respond to the City's discovery requests within fourteen days of the date of this order. Plaintiff shall make herself available for deposition within thirty days of the date of this order. Sanctions against Plaintiff and her attorney are awarded to the City in the amount of $500 and must be submitted to the City within thirty days of the date of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highman-ball.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NICOLE VETZMADIAN VS. ANTONIO R. GARCIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Deft'S Subpoena Of Certain Employment Records
rulings.Rulings: set for hearing on monday, May 21, 2012, Line 5. PLAINTIFF NICOLE VETZMADIAN's Motion To Quash Defendant'S Subpoena Of Certain Employment Records. Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to quash is denied. The subpoenaed documents are relevant to plaintiff's wage loss claim. Defendant has a right to independently verify the evidence that plaintiff provided by subpoenaing it from the employer. The Court is not persuaded that plaintiff's long-term relationship with her employer will be damaged by a subpoena in this car accident case. However, the Court would be inclined to change its tentative and grant the motion if plaintiff stipulates to drop her claim for wage loss damages. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highman-ball.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)
